content:
Hi Tobias

Undo Deletion

The app gives users the ability to display a confirmation prompt before deletion, rather than an Undo feature. This is enabled from: Settings > General Settings > Interactions: Confirm Actions

Folder add/delete/rename

Adding, deleting and renaming folders is not currently supported within the app. This needs to be managed from your email providers web portal or another client.

The best way to suggest and show support for new ideas is through Mozilla Connect.

A feature request similar to your own is linked below and open to votes. https://connect.mozilla.org/t5/ideas/add-the-ability-to-create-imap-folders/idi-p/72777
creator: platform34

content:
The android client does not have any filtering capability.

The idea has been suggested by community members. You can show support by up-voting the idea linked below on Mozilla Connect.

https://connect.mozilla.org/t5/ideas/filtering-with-android-thunderbird/idi-p/61144

An alternative is to apply your filtering rules at the server level where possible. Many email providers support this to varying levels. Server filtering has the advantage of being reflected on all clients, desktop & mobile.

content:
Hi Van

1. You first need to grant Thunderbird permission to access your contacts. Long press Thunderbird's launcher icon > app info > permissions.

2. In Thunderbird: Select settings > general settings > display;

  1. Tick "Show correspondent names".
  2. Tick "Show contact names".

3. To pick an email address from your contacts

  1. Open the message compose screen.
  2. Press the 3 dot menu button (top right).
  3. Press "Add from contacts".

content:
Hi Peter

It's not possible to copy local folders from desktop to the phone.

1. The apps data is stored in the secure area of the phones storage and only accessible with a rooted phone. 2. The way Thunderbird for Android email data is stored is likely very different and incompatible with the desktop application given it's legacy K-9 heritage.

What type of connection to the server are you using, Imap or pop3?

IMAP: The app downloads folders and their content from, and keeps them synchronized with the server. You'll likely need to enable sync on folders beyond the standard ones such as Inbox. This is done by selecting the account > manage folders > [Folder Name]: toggle sync or push.

At the moment, the app don't have the ability to create, delete or rename folders. This currently must be done on the server's web portal or another email client.

POP3: The protocol itself doesn't support the concept of folders. It only downloads new emails within the inbox. Any folder management is done locally on the client by the user.

I don't use POP3 so I'm unable to confirm if Thunderbird for Android supports this.
creator: platform34

content:
The app doesn't have it's own address book. It can pull email addresses from your phone's contact app such as google contacts. To use the feature, you should

1. Grant the permission allowing the app to access your contacts

2. Within the apps settings, navigate to general settings > display - Enable "Show correspondent names" - Enable "Show contact names"

3. To pick a contact's email address on the message compose screen, press the 3 dot menu icon and select "Add from contacts"

ProtonMail n'est pas compatible avec Thunderbird pour Android. Veuillez utiliser l'application officielle ProtonMail.

Pour utiliser ProtonMail avec des clients IMAP/SMTP, l'application ProtonMail Bridge est nécessaire pour gérer le chiffrement et le déchiffrement. Il ne semble pas exister de version mobile de cet outil.

Vous trouverez plus d'informations sur la configuration pour ordinateur via le lien ci-dessous. https://proton.me/support/protonmail-bridge-clients-windows-thunderbird
